9	    },

11	      "id": "q2",
12	      "question": "The backend uses mixed casing conventions: some response fields are snake_case (created_at, expires_at) while others are camelCase (variableId, marketId). The TypeScript types reflect this mix. Should the OpenAPI schema document the actual wire format as-is, or should we normalize naming?",
13	      "category": "codebase",
14	      "why_it_matters": "Schema property names must match the actual JSON wire format for the spec to be useful for code generation and validation. Documenting the mixed convention accurately is critical."
15	    },
16	    {
17	      "id": "q3",
18	      "question": "Several endpoints return different response shapes depending on context (e.g., GET / returns HTML or JSON based on Accept header, POST endpoints return different structures on idempotent replay vs fresh creation). Should we document all response variants or just the primary JSON responses?",
19	      "category": "design",
20	      "why_it_matters": "Documenting every content-negotiation variant and idempotency replay shape increases spec size substantially. The primary use case for the spec determines which variants matter."
21	    },
22	    {
23	      "id": "q4",
24	      "question": "The rate-limiting behavior (X-RateLimit-* headers, 429 responses) is conditionally enabled via BAYES_RATE_LIMIT_PER_MIN env var. Should rate-limiting headers and 429 responses be documented as standard response components even though they're optional?",
25	      "category": "design",
26	      "why_it_matters": "Consumers need to know about rate limiting to build resilient clients. Documenting it as always-present when it's optional could confuse integrators."
27	    },
28	    {
29	      "id": "q5",
30	      "question": "The TypeScript types include several types not directly tied to REST endpoints (e.g., MarketPriceMessage, MarketEventMessage, AccountRiskMessage for WebSocket, Session for client state). Should OpenAPI schemas cover only REST request/response types, or all types from types.ts?",
31	      "category": "requirements",
32	      "why_it_matters": "Including WebSocket message types and client-only types in the schema components bloats the spec with types that don't map to any REST operation, but omitting them means the spec doesn't fully mirror types.ts."
33	    }

63	      "confidence": 0.7,

+++ b/docs/openapi.yaml
@@ -0,0 +1,1885 @@
+openapi: 3.0.3
+info:
+  title: Bayes Market API
+  description: |
+    Prediction market engine with LMSR-based pricing, event-sourced command
+    processing, and real-time marginal probability updates.
+
+    ## Authentication
+
+    Write endpoints require an `X-Bayes-Agent-Id` header when the server is
+    running with `BAYES_REQUIRE_AGENT_ID=true` (the production default).
+    The header value must match the token pattern `[!#$%&'*+\-.^_\`|~0-9A-Za-z]+`.
+
+    ## Rate Limiting
+
+    When `BAYES_RATE_LIMIT_PER_MIN` is set, responses include standard rate
+    limit headers (`X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`,
+    `X-RateLimit-Reset`, `X-RateLimit-Policy`). Exceeding the limit returns
+    `429` with a `Retry-After` header.
+
+    ## WebSocket Endpoints (not covered here)
+
+    Real-time streaming is available at `/ws/markets/{id}/prices`,
+    `/ws/markets/{id}/events`, and `/ws/accounts/{id}/risk`. These are not
+    representable in OpenAPI 3.0 and are excluded from this specification.
+  version: 1.0.0
+  contact:
+    name: Bayes Market Engineering
+
+servers:
+  - url: http://localhost:3205
+    description: Local development server
+
+tags:
+  - name: Health
+    description: Service health and discovery endpoints
+  - name: Markets
+    description: Market CRUD, metadata, analytics, events, and comments
+  - name: Orders
+    description: Trading endpoints (ProbabilityEdit, EventTrade)
+  - name: Accounts
+    description: Account risk, exposure, positions, and P&L
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Paths
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+paths:
+
+  # ---- Health ----
+
+  /:
+    get:
+      tags: [Health]
+      summary: Service index
+      description: Returns the public HTTP surface listing all available route groups.
+      operationId: getServiceIndex
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Service index payload
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                type: object
+                required: [service, status, routes, meta]
+                properties:
+                  service:
+                    type: string
+                    example: bayes-market
+                  status:
+                    type: string
+                    enum: [ok]
+                  routes:
+                    type: object
+                    properties:
+                      health:
+                        type: array
+                        items:
+                          type: string
+                      markets:
+                        type: array
+                        items:
+                          type: string
+                      orders:
+                        type: array
+                        items:
+                          type: string
+                      accounts:
+                        type: array
+                        items:
+                          type: string
+                  meta:
+                    $ref: "#/components/schemas/Meta"
+
+  /health:
+    get:
+      tags: [Health]
+      summary: Legacy health check
+      description: Returns basic service health. Identical to `/healthz`.
+      operationId: getHealth
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Service is healthy
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: "#/components/schemas/HealthResponse"
+
+  /healthz:
+    get:
+      tags: [Health]
+      summary: Legacy health check (alias)
+      description: |
+        Kubernetes-style health probe endpoint. Returns the same payload as
+        `/health`. Kept for backward compatibility with existing monitoring
+        tools.
+      operationId: getHealthz
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Service is healthy
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: "#/components/schemas/HealthResponse"
+
+  /v1/health:
+    get:
+      tags: [Health]
+      summary: Versioned health check with component breakdown
+      description: |
+        Returns detailed health with per-component status for database,
+        inference engine, and authentication subsystems. The top-level
+        `status` is an aggregate: `ok` when all components are healthy,
+        `degraded` when any component is degraded, or `unhealthy` when
+        any component is down.
+      operationId: getV1Health
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Detailed health payload
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: "#/components/schemas/V1HealthResponse"
+
+  # ---- Markets (read) ----
+
+  /v1/markets:
+    get:
+      tags: [Markets]
+      summary: List markets
+      description: |
+        Returns the market collection with optional filtering by status,
+        sorting, and title search. Resolved markets are excluded by default
+        unless `include_resolved=true` or `status=resolved` or `status=all`.
+      operationId: listMarkets
+      parameters:
+        - name: status
+          in: query
+          description: Filter by market status.
+          schema:
+            type: string
+            enum: [active, resolved, closed, draft, all]
+        - name: sort
+          in: query
+          description: Sort order for the results.
+          schema:
+            type: string
+            enum: [volume, liquidity, created]
+        - name: q
+          in: query
+          description: Case-insensitive title substring search.
+          schema:
+            type: string
+        - name: include_resolved
+          in: query
+          description: Include resolved markets in results.
+          schema:
+            type: boolean
+            default: false
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Market list
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                type: object
+                required: [markets, count, meta]
+                properties:
+                  markets:
+                    type: array
+                    items:
+                      $ref: "#/components/schemas/MarketSummary"
+                  count:
+                    type: integer
+                  meta:
+                    $ref: "#/components/schemas/Meta"
+        "400":
+          $ref: "#/components/responses/BadRequest"
+
+    post:
+      tags: [Markets]
+      summary: Create a market
+      operationId: createMarket
+      security:
+        - AgentId: []
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: "#/components/schemas/CreateMarketRequest"
+      responses:
+        "201":
+          description: Market created
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                type: object
+                required: [market, event, meta]
+                properties:
+                  market:
+                    $ref: "#/components/schemas/Market"
+                  event:
+                    $ref: "#/components/schemas/Event"
+                  meta:
+                    $ref: "#/components/schemas/Meta"
+        "400":
+          $ref: "#/components/responses/BadRequest"
+        "401":
+          $ref: "#/components/responses/Unauthorized"
+        "409":
+          description: Market with this title already exists
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ErrorResponse"
+        "429":
+          $ref: "#/components/responses/RateLimited"
+
+  /v1/markets/{marketId}:
+    get:
+      tags: [Markets]
+      summary: Get market detail
+      operationId: getMarket
+      parameters:
+        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/MarketId"
+        - name: context
+          in: query
+          description: |
+            Condition assignments for conditional probability queries.
+            Each value has the format `variableId:outcomeId`.
+          schema:
+            type: array
+            items:
+              type: string
+          style: form
+          explode: true
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Full market payload
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                type: object
+                required: [market, meta]
+                properties:
+                  market:
+                    $ref: "#/components/schemas/Market"
+                  meta:
+                    $ref: "#/components/schemas/Meta"
+        "404":
+          $ref: "#/components/responses/NotFound"
+
+  /v1/markets/{marketId}/meta:
+    get:
+      tags: [Markets]
+      summary: Get market preview metadata
+      description: Returns Open Graph and share-preview metadata for the market.
+      operationId: getMarketPreview
+      parameters:
+        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/MarketId"
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Market preview payload
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                type: object
+                required: [preview, meta]
+                properties:
+                  preview:
+                    $ref: "#/components/schemas/MarketPreview"
+                  meta:
+                    $ref: "#/components/schemas/Meta"
+        "404":
+          $ref: "#/components/responses/NotFound"
+
+  /v1/markets/{marketId}/engine-stats:
+    get:
+      tags: [Markets]
+      summary: Get engine diagnostics
+      description: |
+        Returns inference engine diagnostics for the market including timing
+        statistics, cache hit rates, and junction tree compilation details.
+      operationId: getMarketEngineStats
+      parameters:
+        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/MarketId"
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Engine stats payload
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: "#/components/schemas/EngineStatsResponse"
+        "404":
+          $ref: "#/components/responses/NotFound"
+
+  /v1/markets/{marketId}/analytics:
+    get:
+      tags: [Markets]
+      summary: Get market analytics
+      description: |
+        Returns aggregated analytics for a market: total volume, trade count,
+        time-bucketed price history, and top traders.
+      operationId: getMarketAnalytics
+      parameters:
+        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/MarketId"
+        - name: interval
+          in: query
+          description: Time bucket interval for price history.
+          schema:
+            type: string
+            enum: ["1h", "6h", "1d", "7d"]
+            default: "1h"
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Analytics payload
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: "#/components/schemas/AnalyticsResponse"
+        "400":
+          $ref: "#/components/responses/BadRequest"
+        "404":
+          $ref: "#/components/responses/NotFound"
+
+  /v1/markets/{marketId}/events:
+    get:
+      tags: [Markets]
+      summary: Get market event journal
+      description: Returns the paginated event-sourced journal for a market.
+      operationId: getMarketEvents
+      parameters:
+        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/MarketId"
+        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/FromSeq"
+        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/Limit"
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Paginated event list
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                type: object
+                required: [marketId, events, chain, pagination, meta]
+                properties:
+                  marketId:
+                    type: string
+                  events:
+                    type: array
+                    items:
+                      $ref: "#/components/schemas/Event"
+                  chain:
+                    $ref: "#/components/schemas/ChainState"
+                  pagination:
+                    $ref: "#/components/schemas/SeqPagination"
+                  meta:
+                    $ref: "#/components/schemas/Meta"
+        "404":
+          $ref: "#/components/responses/NotFound"
+
+  /v1/markets/{marketId}/comments:
+    get:
+      tags: [Markets]
+      summary: Get market comments
+      description: Returns the paginated discussion thread for a market.
+      operationId: getMarketComments
+      parameters:
+        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/MarketId"
+        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/FromSeq"
+        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/Limit"
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Paginated comment list
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                type: object
+                required: [marketId, comments, pagination, meta]
+                properties:
+                  marketId:
+                    type: string
+                  comments:
+                    type: array
+                    items:
+                      $ref: "#/components/schemas/Comment"
+                  pagination:
+                    $ref: "#/components/schemas/SeqPagination"
+                  meta:
+                    $ref: "#/components/schemas/Meta"
+        "404":
+          $ref: "#/components/responses/NotFound"
+
+    post:
+      tags: [Markets]
+      summary: Post a comment
+      operationId: postComment
+      security:
+        - AgentId: []
+      parameters:
+        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/MarketId"
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: "#/components/schemas/PostCommentRequest"
+      responses:
+        "201":
+          description: Comment accepted
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                type: object
+                required: [comment, meta]
+                properties:
+                  comment:
+                    $ref: "#/components/schemas/Comment"
+                  meta:
+                    $ref: "#/components/schemas/Meta"
+        "400":
+          $ref: "#/components/responses/BadRequest"
+        "401":
+          $ref: "#/components/responses/Unauthorized"
+        "404":
+          $ref: "#/components/responses/NotFound"
+        "409":
+          description: Market not active or idempotency conflict
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ErrorResponse"
+        "429":
+          $ref: "#/components/responses/RateLimited"
+
+  # ---- Markets (write) ----
+
+  /v1/markets/{marketId}/resolve:
+    post:
+      tags: [Markets]
+      summary: Resolve a market
+      description: |
+        Resolves a market by setting a winning outcome or explicit final
+        probability distribution. Only active or closed markets can be
+        resolved. Provide either `outcomeId` for a binary resolution or
+        `finalProbabilities` for a custom distribution.
+      operationId: resolveMarket
+      security:
+        - AgentId: []
+      parameters:
+        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/MarketId"
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: "#/components/schemas/ResolveMarketRequest"
+      responses:
+        "201":
+          description: Market resolved
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                type: object
+                required: [market, result, meta]
+                properties:
+                  market:
+                    $ref: "#/components/schemas/Market"
+                  result:
+                    $ref: "#/components/schemas/TerminalResult"
+                  meta:
+                    $ref: "#/components/schemas/CommandResponseMeta"
+        "400":
+          $ref: "#/components/responses/BadRequest"
+        "401":
+          $ref: "#/components/responses/Unauthorized"

+    description: Market CRUD, metadata, analytics, events, and comments
+  - name: Orders
+    description: Trading endpoints (ProbabilityEdit, EventTrade)
+  - name: Accounts
+    description: Account risk, exposure, positions, and P&L
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Paths
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+paths:
+
+  # ---- Health ----
+
+  /:
+    get:
+      tags: [Health]
+      summary: Service index
+      description: Returns the public HTTP surface listing all available route groups.
+      operationId: getServiceIndex
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Service index payload
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                type: object
+                required: [service, status, routes, meta]
+                properties:
+                  service:
+                    type: string
+                    example: bayes-market
+                  status:
+                    type: string
+                    enum: [ok]
+                  routes:
+                    type: object
+                    properties:
+                      health:
+                        type: array
+                        items:
+                          type: string
+                      markets:
+                        type: array
+                        items:
+                          type: string
+                      orders:
+                        type: array
+                        items:
+                          type: string
+                      accounts:
+                        type: array
+                        items:
+                          type: string
+                  meta:
+                    $ref: "#/components/schemas/Meta"
+
+  /health:
+    get:
+      tags: [Health]
+      summary: Legacy health check
+      description: Returns basic service health. Identical to `/healthz`.
+      operationId: getHealth
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Service is healthy
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: "#/components/schemas/HealthResponse"
+
+  /healthz:
+    get:
+      tags: [Health]
+      summary: Legacy health check (alias)
+      description: |
+        Kubernetes-style health probe endpoint. Returns the same payload as
+        `/health`. Kept for backward compatibility with existing monitoring
+        tools.
+      operationId: getHealthz
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Service is healthy
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: "#/components/schemas/HealthResponse"
+
+  /v1/health:
+    get:
+      tags: [Health]
+      summary: Versioned health check with component breakdown
+      description: |
+        Returns detailed health with per-component status for database,
+        inference engine, and authentication subsystems. The top-level
+        `status` is an aggregate: `ok` when all components are healthy,
+        `degraded` when any component is degraded, or `unhealthy` when
+        any component is down.
+      operationId: getV1Health
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Detailed health payload
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: "#/components/schemas/V1HealthResponse"
+
+  # ---- Markets (read) ----
+
+  /v1/markets:
+    get:
+      tags: [Markets]
+      summary: List markets
+      description: |
+        Returns the market collection with optional filtering by status,
+        sorting, and title search. Resolved markets are excluded by default
+        unless `include_resolved=true` or `status=resolved` or `status=all`.
+      operationId: listMarkets
+      parameters:
+        - name: status
+          in: query
+          description: Filter by market status.
+          schema:
+            type: string
+            enum: [active, resolved, closed, draft, all]
+        - name: sort
+          in: query
+          description: Sort order for the results.
+          schema:
+            type: string
+            enum: [volume, liquidity, created]
+        - name: q
+          in: query
+          description: Case-insensitive title substring search.
+          schema:
+            type: string
+        - name: include_resolved
+          in: query
+          description: Include resolved markets in results.
+          schema:
+            type: boolean
+            default: false
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Market list
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                type: object
+                required: [markets, count, meta]
+                properties:
+                  markets:
+                    type: array
+                    items:
+                      $ref: "#/components/schemas/MarketSummary"
+                  count:
+                    type: integer
+                  meta:
+                    $ref: "#/components/schemas/Meta"
+        "400":
+          $ref: "#/components/responses/BadRequest"
+
+    post:
+      tags: [Markets]
+      summary: Create a market
+      operationId: createMarket
+      security:
+        - AgentId: []
+      requestBody:
+        required: true
+        content:
+          application/json:
+            schema:
+              $ref: "#/components/schemas/CreateMarketRequest"
+      responses:
+        "201":
+          description: Market created
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                type: object
+                required: [market, event, meta]
+                properties:
+                  market:
+                    $ref: "#/components/schemas/Market"
+                  event:
+                    $ref: "#/components/schemas/Event"
+                  meta:
+                    $ref: "#/components/schemas/Meta"
+        "400":
+          $ref: "#/components/responses/BadRequest"
+        "401":
+          $ref: "#/components/responses/Unauthorized"
+        "409":
+          description: Market with this title already exists
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ErrorResponse"
+        "429":
+          $ref: "#/components/responses/RateLimited"
+
+  /v1/markets/{marketId}:
+    get:
+      tags: [Markets]
+      summary: Get market detail
+      operationId: getMarket
+      parameters:
+        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/MarketId"
+        - name: context
+          in: query
+          description: |
+            Condition assignments for conditional probability queries.
+            Each value has the format `variableId:outcomeId`.
+          schema:
+            type: array
+            items:
+              type: string
+          style: form
+          explode: true
+      responses:
+        "200":
+          description: Full market payload
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                type: object

+        "404":

5	    Prediction market engine with LMSR-based pricing, event-sourced command
6	    processing, and real-time marginal probability updates.
7	
8	    ## Authentication
9	
10	    Write endpoints require an `X-Bayes-Agent-Id` header when the server is
11	    running with `BAYES_REQUIRE_AGENT_ID=true` (the production default).
12	    The header value must match the token pattern `[!#$%&'*+\-.^_\`|~0-9A-Za-z]+`.
13	
14	    ## Rate Limiting
15	
16	    When `BAYES_RATE_LIMIT_PER_MIN` is set, responses include standard rate
17	    limit headers (`X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`,
18	    `X-RateLimit-Reset`, `X-RateLimit-Policy`). Exceeding the limit returns
19	    `429` with a `Retry-After` header.
20	
21	    ## WebSocket Endpoints (not covered here)
22	
23	    Real-time streaming is available at `/ws/markets/{id}/prices`,
24	    `/ws/markets/{id}/events`, and `/ws/accounts/{id}/risk`. These are not
25	    representable in OpenAPI 3.0 and are excluded from this specification.

## VALIDATION REPORT: OpenAPI Schema vs TypeScript Types

### PART 1: OpenAPI Schemas (from docs/openapi.yaml)

### PART 2: TypeScript Types (from frontend/src/lib/api/types.ts)

**REST-relevant interfaces/types to validate** (excluding WebSocket message types and client-only types):

1. **MarketOutcome** - id, name
2. **MarketSummary** - id, title, status, liquidity, volume, expires_at
3. **Market** - id, title, description, variableId, status, resolution?, resolutionProbabilities?, outcomes, marginals, liquidity, volume, created_at, expires_at
4. **MarketStatus** - type alias
5. **MarketSortField** - type alias
6. **MarketListFilters** - status?, includeResolved?, sort?, q?
7. **MarketListFilterInput** - type alias
8. **MarketListResponseMeta** - extends Meta; filters
9. **MarketListResponse** - markets, count, meta
10. **MarketDetailResponse** - market, meta
11. **MarketPreview** - marketId, title, description, url, siteName, type, outcomes?
12. **MarketPreviewResponse** - preview, meta
13. **PriceHistoryEntry** - timestamp, marginals
14. **TopTrader** - account_id, volume, trade_count
15. **MarketAnalyticsResponse** - market_id, total_volume, trade_count, price_history, top_traders, interval, meta
16. **MarketEvent** - eventId, marketId, seq, type, prevEventHash, eventHash, timestamp, payload
17. **MarketEventsResponse** - events, meta
18. **MarketComment** - commentId, marketId, seq, accountId, body, createdAt
19. **PaginatedCollection** - fromSeq, limit, returned, nextFromSeq
20. **MarketCommentsResponse** - marketId, comments, pagination, meta
21. **EngineInfo** - mode, backend, version, precision, compile_id, compile_type, source_state_hash
22. **CliqueSummary** - id, nodes, size, states
23. **EngineStatsResponse** - marketId, engine, cliques, diagnostics, meta
24. **MarketRisk** - marketId, minAsset, capacityConsumed, utilization, commandCount, lastOrderId, lastCommandId, updatedAt
25. **CapacityIndicators** - limit, available, consumed, utilization, status
26. **AccountRiskResponse** - account { id, risk }, meta
27. **AccountExposurePosition** - marketId, outcomeId, netSize, absSize, lastTradePrice, updatedAt, lastOrderId?, lastCommandId?
28. **AccountExposureResponse** - account { id, exposure }, meta
29. **AssetDelta** - beforeMinAsset, afterMinAsset, impactScore, riskLimit
30. **TerminalOutcome** - status, reason?, code?
31. **OrderResponse** - command, order, assetDelta, terminalOutcome, meta
32. **AcceptedTerminalResult** - terminal, status, eventType, eventId, commandId, emittedAt
33. **CommandResponseMeta** - extends Meta, idempotencyKeyEcho?, replayed?
34. **ApiError** - error { code, message, details? }, meta

---

### PART 3: DISCREPANCIES

#### **MISSING SCHEMAS IN OPENAPI:**

1. **MarketOutcome** - Not present in OpenAPI, only "Outcome" exists (different name)
2. **MarketListFilters** - Not present in OpenAPI
3. **MarketListFilterInput** - Not present in OpenAPI
4. **MarketListResponseMeta** - Not present in OpenAPI (filters structure not documented)
5. **MarketListResponse** - Not present in OpenAPI
6. **MarketDetailResponse** - Not present in OpenAPI
7. **MarketPreviewResponse** - Not present in OpenAPI
8. **PriceHistoryEntry** - Not present in OpenAPI (inlined in AnalyticsResponse)
9. **TopTrader** - Not present in OpenAPI (inlined in AnalyticsResponse)
10. **MarketAnalyticsResponse** - Present as "AnalyticsResponse" (different name)
11. **MarketEvent** - Not present in OpenAPI (present as "Event" with different fields)
12. **MarketEventsResponse** - Not present in OpenAPI
13. **MarketComment** - Present as "Comment" (different name, same fields)
14. **PaginatedCollection** - Not present in OpenAPI (present as "SeqPagination" with different fields)
15. **MarketCommentsResponse** - Not present in OpenAPI
16. **EngineInfo** - Not present in OpenAPI (inlined in EngineStatsResponse)
17. **CliqueSummary** - Not present in OpenAPI (inlined in EngineStatsResponse)
18. **AccountRiskResponse** - Not present in OpenAPI
19. **AccountExposureResponse** - Not present in OpenAPI
20. **AccountExposurePosition** - Not present in OpenAPI (present as "ExposurePosition")
21. **AssetDelta** - Not present in OpenAPI
22. **TerminalOutcome** - Not present in OpenAPI
23. **OrderResponse** - Not present in OpenAPI
24. **AcceptedTerminalResult** - Not present in OpenAPI (present as "TerminalResult" with broader scope)
25. **ApiError** - Not present in OpenAPI
26. **ProbabilityEditPayload** - Not present in OpenAPI
27. **EventTradeOrderPayload** - Not present in OpenAPI
28. **EventTradeOrder** - Not present in OpenAPI (inlined in EventTradeResponse)
29. **CommentPayload** - Not present in OpenAPI
30. **CommentResponse** - Not present in OpenAPI
31. **MarketPnlSummary** - Present as "PnlSummary" (different name)
32. **MarketPnlResponse** - Not present in OpenAPI
33. **AccountPnlMarket** - Not present in OpenAPI (inlined in AccountPnl)
34. **AccountPnlResponse** - Not present in OpenAPI

#### **SCHEMA NAMING MISMATCHES:**

| TypeScript | OpenAPI | Issue |
|-----------|---------|-------|
| MarketOutcome | Outcome | Different name; OpenAPI "Outcome" is generic |
| MarketComment | Comment | Different name |
| MarketAnalyticsResponse | AnalyticsResponse | Different name |
| MarketPnlSummary | PnlSummary | Different name |
| MarketRisk | MarketRisk | Match ✓ |
| CapacityIndicators | CapacityIndicators | Match ✓ |
| OutcomePnl | OutcomePnl | Match ✓ |

#### **FIELD-LEVEL DISCREPANCIES:**

**1. Market schema:**
- OpenAPI has `maxPositionSize` field (optional) that TypeScript doesn't include
- TypeScript has `resolution` (optional) and `resolutionProbabilities` (optional) ✓

**2. MarketPreview schema:**
- OpenAPI requires: `og_description`, `image`
- TypeScript interface doesn't include these fields
- TypeScript has `outcomes?` as optional array of objects with `{ id, name, probability }`
- OpenAPI requires `outcomes` with same structure ✓

**3. EngineStatsResponse schema:**
- OpenAPI has nested `engine` object with fields: mode, backend, version, precision, compile_id, compile_type, source_state_hash (all required)
- TypeScript has separate `EngineInfo` interface with same fields
- OpenAPI `diagnostics.inference` has `count` field (required); TypeScript has `sample_count` instead ✗
- OpenAPI `diagnostics` optional fields: `compile_time_ms`, `memory_bytes`, `last_updated`
- TypeScript doesn't include these optional fields

**4. AnalyticsResponse vs MarketAnalyticsResponse:**
- Field names match but OpenAPI calls it "AnalyticsResponse" not "MarketAnalyticsResponse"
- OpenAPI has embedded types for price_history items and top_traders items
- TypeScript has separate `PriceHistoryEntry` and `TopTrader` interfaces (better design)

**5. AccountRisk in OpenAPI:**
- Called "AccountRisk" but TypeScript has "AccountRiskResponse" wrapping it
- OpenAPI structure: { minAssets: { overall, markets: [] }, capacityIndicators, updatedAt }
- TypeScript AccountRiskResponse has: account: { id, risk: { minAssets: { overall, markets: [] }, capacityIndicators, updatedAt } }, meta

**6. ExposurePosition vs AccountExposurePosition:**
- OpenAPI has `maxPositionSize` field; TypeScript doesn't include it
- TypeScript has `lastOrderId` and `lastCommandId` (both nullable); OpenAPI also has them (both nullable) ✓

**7. PaginatedCollection vs SeqPagination:**
- OpenAPI: `fromSeq`, `limit`, `returned`, `nextFromSeq` (nullable)
- TypeScript: `fromSeq`, `limit`, `returned`, `nextFromSeq` (nullable) - identical ✓

**8. Event schema in OpenAPI vs MarketEvent in TypeScript:**
- OpenAPI Event has: `schemaVersion`, `eventId`, `marketId`, `seq`, `commandId`, `eventType`, `emittedAt`, `approxFlag`, `payload`, `prevEventHash`, `eventHash`
- TypeScript MarketEvent has: `eventId`, `marketId`, `seq`, `type` (not eventType), `prevEventHash`, `eventHash`, `timestamp` (not emittedAt), `payload`
- Missing in OpenAPI Event: `type` field renamed to `eventType`

**9. CommandResponseMeta:**

**10. TerminalResult in OpenAPI vs TerminalOutcome & AcceptedTerminalResult in TypeScript:**

| Missing fields in TypeScript interfaces | 2 | maxPositionSize (Market, ExposurePosition), compile_time_ms/memory_bytes (EngineStats) |

**Overall Assessment:** The OpenAPI spec is **significantly incomplete** compared to TypeScript types. Many response envelopes and request payloads are missing from the spec, and several schemas have field mismatches, particularly around naming conventions and optional field handling.

**REST-relevant interfaces/types to validate** (excluding WebSocket message types and client-only types):

1. **MarketOutcome** - id, name
2. **MarketSummary** - id, title, status, liquidity, volume, expires_at
3. **Market** - id, title, description, variableId, status, resolution?, resolutionProbabilities?, outcomes, marginals, liquidity, volume, created_at, expires_at
4. **MarketStatus** - type alias
5. **MarketSortField** - type alias

45. **MarketPnlResponse** - pnl { marketId, outcomes, summary }, meta

47. **AccountPnlResponse** - pnl { accountId, markets, summary }, meta

---

### PART 3: DISCREPANCIES

#### **MISSING SCHEMAS IN OPENAPI:**

1. **MarketOutcome** - Not present in OpenAPI, only "Outcome" exists (different name)
2. **MarketListFilters** - Not present in OpenAPI
3. **MarketListFilterInput** - Not present in OpenAPI
4. **MarketListResponseMeta** - Not present in OpenAPI (filters structure not documented)
5. **MarketListResponse** - Not present in OpenAPI
6. **MarketDetailResponse** - Not present in OpenAPI
7. **MarketPreviewResponse** - Not present in OpenAPI
8. **PriceHistoryEntry** - Not present in OpenAPI (inlined in AnalyticsResponse)
9. **TopTrader** - Not present in OpenAPI (inlined in AnalyticsResponse)
10. **MarketAnalyticsResponse** - Present as "AnalyticsResponse" (different name)
11. **MarketEvent** - Not present in OpenAPI (present as "Event" with different fields)
12. **MarketEventsResponse** - Not present in OpenAPI
13. **MarketComment** - Present as "Comment" (different name, same fields)
14. **PaginatedCollection** - Not present in OpenAPI (present as "SeqPagination" with different fields)
15. **MarketCommentsResponse** - Not present in OpenAPI
16. **EngineInfo** - Not present in OpenAPI (inlined in EngineStatsResponse)
17. **CliqueSummary** - Not present in OpenAPI (inlined in EngineStatsResponse)
18. **AccountRiskResponse** - Not present in OpenAPI
19. **AccountExposureResponse** - Not present in OpenAPI
20. **AccountExposurePosition** - Not present in OpenAPI (present as "ExposurePosition")
21. **AssetDelta** - Not present in OpenAPI
22. **TerminalOutcome** - Not present in OpenAPI
23. **OrderResponse** - Not present in OpenAPI
24. **AcceptedTerminalResult** - Not present in OpenAPI (present as "TerminalResult" with broader scope)
25. **ApiError** - Not present in OpenAPI
26. **ProbabilityEditPayload** - Not present in OpenAPI
27. **EventTradeOrderPayload** - Not present in OpenAPI

32. **MarketPnlResponse** - Not present in OpenAPI
33. **AccountPnlMarket** - Not present in OpenAPI (inlined in AccountPnl)
34. **AccountPnlResponse** - Not present in OpenAPI

#### **SCHEMA NAMING MISMATCHES:**

| TypeScript | OpenAPI | Issue |
|-----------|---------|-------|
| MarketOutcome | Outcome | Different name; OpenAPI "Outcome" is generic |
| MarketComment | Comment | Different name |
| MarketAnalyticsResponse | AnalyticsResponse | Different name |
| MarketPnlSummary | PnlSummary | Different name |
| MarketRisk | MarketRisk | Match ✓ |
| CapacityIndicators | CapacityIndicators | Match ✓ |
| OutcomePnl | OutcomePnl | Match ✓ |

#### **FIELD-LEVEL DISCREPANCIES:**

**1. Market schema:**
- OpenAPI has `maxPositionSize` field (optional) that TypeScript doesn't include
- TypeScript has `resolution` (optional) and `resolutionProbabilities` (optional) ✓

**2. MarketPreview schema:**
- OpenAPI requires: `og_description`, `image`
- TypeScript interface doesn't include these fields
- TypeScript has `outcomes?` as optional array of objects with `{ id, name, probability }`
- OpenAPI requires `outcomes` with same structure ✓

**3. EngineStatsResponse schema:**
- OpenAPI has nested `engine` object with fields: mode, backend, version, precision, compile_id, compile_type, source_state_hash (all required)
- TypeScript has separate `EngineInfo` interface with same fields
- OpenAPI `diagnostics.inference` has `count` field (required); TypeScript has `sample_count` instead ✗
- OpenAPI `diagnostics` optional fields: `compile_time_ms`, `memory_bytes`, `last_updated`
- TypeScript doesn't include these optional fields

**4. AnalyticsResponse vs MarketAnalyticsResponse:**
- Field names match but OpenAPI calls it "AnalyticsResponse" not "MarketAnalyticsResponse"
- OpenAPI has embedded types for price_history items and top_traders items
- TypeScript has separate `PriceHistoryEntry` and `TopTrader` interfaces (better design)

**5. AccountRisk in OpenAPI:**
- Called "AccountRisk" but TypeScript has "AccountRiskResponse" wrapping it
- OpenAPI structure: { minAssets: { overall, markets: [] }, capacityIndicators, updatedAt }
- TypeScript AccountRiskResponse has: account: { id, risk: { minAssets: { overall, markets: [] }, capacityIndicators, updatedAt } }, meta

**6. ExposurePosition vs AccountExposurePosition:**
- OpenAPI has `maxPositionSize` field; TypeScript doesn't include it
- TypeScript has `lastOrderId` and `lastCommandId` (both nullable); OpenAPI also has them (both nullable) ✓

**7. PaginatedCollection vs SeqPagination:**
- OpenAPI: `fromSeq`, `limit`, `returned`, `nextFromSeq` (nullable)
- TypeScript: `fromSeq`, `limit`, `returned`, `nextFromSeq` (nullable) - identical ✓

**8. Event schema in OpenAPI vs MarketEvent in TypeScript:**
- OpenAPI Event has: `schemaVersion`, `eventId`, `marketId`, `seq`, `commandId`, `eventType`, `emittedAt`, `approxFlag`, `payload`, `prevEventHash`, `eventHash`
- TypeScript MarketEvent has: `eventId`, `marketId`, `seq`, `type` (not eventType), `prevEventHash`, `eventHash`, `timestamp` (not emittedAt), `payload`
- Missing in OpenAPI Event: `type` field renamed to `eventType`
- Different field names: `timestamp` vs `emittedAt`, `type` vs `eventType`
- OpenAPI has extra fields: `schemaVersion`, `commandId`, `approxFlag` ✗

**9. CommandResponseMeta:**
- OpenAPI: `allOf` structure with Meta + optional `idempotencyKeyEcho`, `replayed`
- TypeScript: extends Meta with optional fields ✓

**10. TerminalResult in OpenAPI vs TerminalOutcome & AcceptedTerminalResult in TypeScript:**
- OpenAPI TerminalResult: broad enum for status (accepted, rejected), fields for rejection reason
- TypeScript AcceptedTerminalResult: status fixed to "accepted", eventType fixed to "CommandAccepted"
- TypeScript TerminalOutcome: has status (accepted, rejected), optional reason and code ✗

**11. EventTradeResponse:**
- OpenAPI has `order` object with embedded payload; TypeScript has `EventTradeOrder` with detailed fields
- OpenAPI missing: `targetMarketId`, `targetOutcomeId` fields that are in TypeScript ✗

**12. ProbabilityEditResponse:**
- OpenAPI order object has: `id`, `type`, `marketId`, `accountId`, `status`, `payload`, `previousMarginals`, `newMarginals`, `impactScore`, `commandId`, `submittedAt`, `createdAt`, `filledAt`, `idempotencyKey`
- TypeScript doesn't document this response structure separately ✗

---

### SUMMARY OF CRITICAL DISCREPANCIES:

| Category | Count | Examples |
|----------|-------|----------|
| Missing response envelope schemas | 15+ | MarketListResponse, MarketDetailResponse, MarketCommentsResponse, OrderResponse, etc. |
| Missing payload schemas | 5 | ProbabilityEditPayload, EventTradePayload, CommentPayload, AssetDelta, etc. |
| Schema naming mismatches | 4 | MarketOutcome→Outcome, MarketAnalyticsResponse→AnalyticsResponse, etc. |
| Field name mismatches | 3 | type↔eventType, timestamp↔emittedAt, sample_count↔count |
| Missing fields in OpenAPI | 5+ | targetMarketId, targetOutcomeId (EventTrade), og_description, image (MarketPreview) |
| Missing fields in TypeScript interfaces | 2 | maxPositionSize (Market, ExposurePosition), compile_time_ms/memory_bytes (EngineStats) |
| Type structure mismatches | 4 | Event schema completeness, TerminalResult vs TerminalOutcome/AcceptedTerminalResult |

**Overall Assessment:** The OpenAPI spec is **significantly incomplete** compared to TypeScript types. Many response envelopes and request payloads are missing from the spec, and several schemas have field mismatches, particularly around naming conventions and optional field handling.

EventTradeResponse:
      type: object
      required: [order, result, meta]
      properties:
        order:
          type: object
          required: [id, type, marketId, accountId, status, payload, notional, price, side, size, commandId, submittedAt, createdAt, filledAt]
          properties:
            id:
              type: string
              example: ord_01J...
            type:
              type: string
              enum: [EventTrade]
            marketId:
              type: string
            accountId:
              type: string
            status:
              type: string
              enum: [filled]
            payload:
              type: object
              additionalProperties: true
            notional:
              type: number
              format: double
              description: Total notional value of the trade.
            price:
              type: number
              format: double
              description: Effective price per share.
            side:
              type: string
              enum: [buy, sell]
            size:
              type: number
              format: double
            commandId:
              type: string
            submittedAt:
              type: string
              format: date-time
            createdAt:
              type: string
              format: date-time
            filledAt:
              type: string
              format: date-time
            idempotencyKey:
              type: string
              nullable: true
        result:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/TerminalResult"
        meta:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/CommandResponseMeta"

    TerminalResult:
      type: object
      description: |
        Terminal outcome block returned by command endpoints. Present on
        both accepted and rejected terminal responses.
      required: [terminal, status, eventType, eventId, commandId, emittedAt]
      properties:
        terminal:
          type: boolean
          enum: [true]
        status:
          type: string
